title: 2026 01 05 Rankett Infrastructure
html:
TL;DR

  • The longest Rankett strategy doc in the vault — "The Grand Slam" / Rankett Partner Program — drafted 2 days before the Jan 7 meetup prep and 6 days before the Jan 11 revision. This is the infrastructure layer the meetup briefings reference.
  • Codifies the $998/seat agency wholesale price with the "$2–4k/month per client" margin pitch, and adds a five-tier offer ladder: 60-Day Sprint, Margin Multiplier, Performance Partnership, Quick-Win + Upgrade, and the 3-Year Lock.
  • Introduces the "5 agencies per month" hard acceptance cap, the 60 day activation rule (with permanent ineligibility on failure), the partner quiz, and explicit "no work without activation" + "no refunds for completed periods" terms.
  • Names the 100% ghost white label mechanic by which the agency's clients never know Rankett exists, and the Partner Proof Library — system-level testimonials/screenshots agencies legally re-brand as their own.
  • The doc closes with a self-flagged risk note: "You carry all the fulfillment risk. If partners sell bad fits, you still have to fulfill for $998 and eat the operational pain" — Yahir naming his own Achilles heel before launch.

Core content

This is a long-form GTM bible covering: the math, the white-label spec, partner scarcity & community, the 5-step partner-activation workflow (profile → 5-min training video → quiz → payment method → "Add Client" unlocks), partner T&Cs, what gets done on the backend per-client per-month, and five distinct Copy-n-Paste agency offers. It also contains a sales kit (Script 1: close the agency owner; Script 2: client sell-sheet; Script 3: monthly upsell talk-track; Script 4: monthly update template) and a 5-minute onboarding portal flow with a non-negotiable intake checklist.

Two structural choices stand out: (1) the offer ladder maps to agency archetype (local services → Margin Multiplier; ecommerce → 60-Day Sprint; B2B/SaaS → Margin Multiplier; solo operator → Quick-Win), so a single sales conversation routes to a different SKU. (2) The Performance-Based Pricing section reframes the $998 as zero-upfront — agencies don't pay to "have access," only when they close. This kills the tire-kicker failure mode the doc explicitly worries about.

The Partner Proof Library is the most interesting compliance maneuver: agencies use language like "our fulfillment engine has produced these outcomes" (truthful) rather than "one of our recent clients..." (false-attribution). Yahir is engineering legal cover for day-one credibility.

Stub-promotion notes

  • whitelabel strategy — already exists; this doc is a strong densify target.
  • ai visibility engine, ghost white label, 60 day activation rule, pooled data network effect, ai search optimization — all exist; reinforced.
  • Partner Proof Library — concept fragment, queued to Potential Entities (single doc).

Privacy

  • No PII; no credentials; no phone numbers; no PII-bearing client names. Sales scripts use placeholders ([Client Name], [Your Agency Name]).

Connections
